name: Gonadorelin
subtitle: Gonadotropin-Releasing Hormone | GnRH Agonist
overview: Gonadorelin is a synthetic form of the naturally occurring gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH). It stimulates the pituitary gland to release follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and luteinizing hormone (LH), which in turn regulate testosterone production in men and ovulation in women. It is FDA-approved for diagnostic testing and fertility treatment. The pulsatile nature of GnRH secretion is critical for proper reproductive function.
mechanism: Gonadorelin acts as an agonist of the GnRH receptor in the anterior pituitary. When administered in pulses (mimicking natural secretion patterns every 60-120 minutes), it stimulates release of FSH and LH. LH then stimulates Leydig cells in the testes to produce testosterone, or triggers ovulation in women. Continuous exposure paradoxically causes receptor downregulation and suppressed hormone production.
keyBenefits: Maintains testicular function during hormone therapy
FDA-approved for diagnostic and therapeutic use
Can restore LH and FSH pulsatility
Alternative to hCG for testicular stimulation

items.name: Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism
items.description: Treatment of conditions like Kallmann syndrome where GnRH deficiency causes low testosterone and infertility.

items.name: Ovulation Induction
items.description: Induces ovulation in women with hypothalamic amenorrhea who don't ovulate regularly.

items.name: Male Fertility
items.description: Stimulates testosterone and sperm production by restoring LH/FSH pulsatility.

items.name: Delayed Puberty
items.description: Treatment of delayed puberty due to hypothalamic-pituitary dysfunction.

items.name: Pituitary Function Testing
items.description: Used to assess hypothalamic-pituitary function and diagnose GnRH deficiency.

items.name: LH/FSH Response Assessment
items.description: Evaluates pituitary reserve and responsiveness to GnRH stimulation.

items.name: TRT Support
items.description: Maintains testicular function and fertility during testosterone replacement therapy.

items.name: Post-Cycle Therapy
items.description: Helps restore natural testosterone production after anabolic steroid use.

items.name: Cryptorchidism
items.description: Treatment of undescended testes in pediatric patients.

deliveryMethods.overview: Can be administered as single injections or via pulsatile infusion pump. Single injections are used for diagnostic testing, while pulsatile administration mimics natural GnRH secretion for therapeutic purposes. Very short half-life (10-40 minutes) requires careful dosing strategy.

steps: Clean work area and hands thoroughly
Inject slowly down vial side (not directly onto powder)
Store in refrigerator, use within 14 days

keyBenefits: Do NOT use continuously (causes receptor downregulation)

deliveryMethods.overview: Nasal spray formulation available in some regions for convenience of administration.

interactions.notes: Often used as alternative to hCG for testicular stimulation during TRT.
Used alongside TRT to maintain testicular function and fertility.
Can be combined in fertility and PCT protocols.
Kisspeptin stimulates GnRH release; can work together for hormone optimization.

timeline.effects: Testosterone increase in response to LH
Improved hormonal markers on blood tests

stopSigns: Signs of allergic reaction (rash, difficulty breathing)
contraindications: Conditions worsened by sex hormone release

references.title: Gonadorelin in Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism
references.keyFindings: Pulsatile GnRH administration effectively restores fertility in patients with hypothalamic hypogonadism.

references.title: GnRH Pulsatility and Reproductive Function
references.keyFindings: Pulsatile GnRH secretion (every 60-120 min) is essential for proper FSH/LH release and reproductive function.

references.title: Gonadorelin for Ovulation Induction
references.keyFindings: Effective treatment for ovulation induction in women with hypothalamic amenorrhea.

quickStats.typicalDose: 100-200 mcg for single injections; 5-20 mcg per pulse for pulsatile therapy
quickStats.frequency: 2-3 times weekly for fertility/TRT support; every 90-120 minutes for pulsatile therapy; single dose for diagnostic
quickStats.cycleDuration: Varies - diagnostic single use, fertility therapy ongoing, TRT support continuous
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: 2-8°C refrigerated; Reconstituted: 2-8°C refrigerated, use within 14 days due to short stability
